# wiki-mcp

> MCP server for markdown-based personal wikis with hybrid search (fulltext + semantic)

## What this is

wiki-mcp is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that lets AI assistants read, write, search, and manage a directory of markdown files as a personal wiki. It combines ripgrep full-text search with sentence-transformer embedding search, merged via Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

## Tools provided

- wiki_get(path, offset?, limit?) — read a file, with optional line pagination
- wiki_get_batch(paths) — read multiple files at once
- wiki_write(path, content) — create or overwrite a file; auto-indexes for search; validates [[wiki links]]
- wiki_append(path, content) — append to an existing file
- wiki_delete(path) — delete a file and remove from search index
- wiki_list(dir?) — list .md files under a directory
- wiki_search(query, mode?, dir?, k?) — search files; mode is "text", "semantic", or "hybrid" (default)
- wiki_replace(old, new, dir?) — find and replace across wiki files
- wiki_links(path) — parse [[links]] in a file and check if targets exist
- wiki_resolve(name) — resolve a wiki link name to a file path

## Search

Three modes:
- text: ripgrep search on file names and content
- semantic: cosine similarity using all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings in sqlite-vec
- hybrid (default): both text and semantic, merged with RRF (k=60)

Files under 10KB get one embedding. Larger files are split by H1/H2 headings, one embedding per section.

## Setup

Requires: Python 3.10+, ripgrep. Docker image: ghcr.io/jhleee/md-mcp-doc

### stdio mode (default)
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "wiki": {
      "command": "docker",
      "args": ["run", "--rm", "-i", "-v", "/path/to/wiki:/wiki", "-e", "WIKI_PATH=/wiki", "ghcr.io/jhleee/md-mcp-doc"]
    }
  }
}
```

### SSE server mode
```bash
docker run -d -v /path/to/wiki:/wiki -e WIKI_PATH=/wiki -e WIKI_TRANSPORT=sse -p 8000:8000 ghcr.io/jhleee/md-mcp-doc
```
```json
{ "mcpServers": { "wiki": { "url": "http://localhost:8000/sse" } } }
```

### Streamable HTTP mode
```bash
docker run -d -v /path/to/wiki:/wiki -e WIKI_PATH=/wiki -e WIKI_TRANSPORT=streamable-http -p 8000:8000 ghcr.io/jhleee/md-mcp-doc
```
```json
{ "mcpServers": { "wiki": { "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp" } } }
```

Environment variables:
- WIKI_PATH (required): path to the wiki directory
- WIKI_DB_PATH: sqlite-vec database path (default: {WIKI_PATH}/.wiki.db)
- WIKI_EMBED_MODEL: embedding model (default: all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
- WIKI_RG_PATH: ripgrep binary path (default: rg)
- WIKI_CHUNK_THRESHOLD: chunking threshold in bytes (default: 10240)
- WIKI_TRANSPORT: transport mode — stdio, sse, or streamable-http (default: stdio)
- WIKI_HOST: bind host for sse/http modes (default: 0.0.0.0)
- WIKI_PORT: bind port for sse/http modes (default: 8000)
- WIKI_AUTH_USER: Basic Auth username; set together with WIKI_AUTH_PASS to enable auth (sse/http only)
- WIKI_AUTH_PASS: Basic Auth password; set together with WIKI_AUTH_USER to enable auth (sse/http only)

## Basic Authentication

Set WIKI_AUTH_USER and WIKI_AUTH_PASS to enable HTTP Basic Auth on sse or streamable-http transports. stdio is unaffected. Credentials are compared in constant time. Use HTTPS (reverse proxy) when exposing over a network.

## Stack

- Python MCP SDK (FastMCP)
- ripgrep for full-text search
- sqlite-vec for vector storage
- sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384-dim, CPU)
- Reciprocal Rank Fusion for hybrid ranking
